pi_items.verified_value: Integrated optical density (IntDen) with background correction is the standard measurement in ImageJ gel analysis
pi_items.source: Confirmed in ImageJ documentation and gel analysis protocols. The GelAnalyzer tool measures integrated density (product of Area and Mean Gray Value) with background subtraction via rolling-ball or baseline subtraction methods.
Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 15) and README.md (line 49). Directory structure created by setup_task.sh line 11.

privileged_info_summary: The Gel sample is a real (non-synthetic) gel electrophoresis image derived from an actual experimental gel photograph, available as a built-in sample in Fiji via File > Open Samples > Gel. It is used as the canonical teaching example for gel analysis in ImageJ documentation. The task requires measuring band intensities using background-corrected integrated optical density (IntDen), a standard densitometry metric in gel analysis. Results must be saved to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/gel_quantification.csv. The image is expected to be in 8-bit grayscale format (standard for gel analysis). Specific lane and band counts in the sample image could not be independently verified from publicly available documentation.
